auth.loginTitle: A barbershop with serious control from day one.
auth.loginDescription: Sign in against the real backend. The JWT is stored in an httpOnly cookie and the dashboard runs as a Server Component.
auth.signupTitle: Create tenant, initial branch and admin in one step.
auth.signupDescription: Signup delegates the whole onboarding flow to the backend and opens the session automatically with the frontend secure cookie.
auth.loginCardDescription: Sign in with email + password. The backend resolves the tenant from the user.
auth.signupCardDescription: This flow creates the tenant, branch, admin and initial access. When it finishes, you land directly in the dashboard.
auth.signupFootnote: Signup calls the real backend and keeps you authenticated without exposing the token in the browser.
auth.sessionExpired: Your session expired. Sign in again.

appointments.title: Scheduling is on the way
appointments.subtitle: The shell already has a place for it and the experience is ready to become the daily operating hub.
appointments.description: Bookings, availability and quick reschedules will land here without forcing a chrome redesign later.

bullets.one: A barber timeline with quick edits and a clean day view.
bullets.two: Availability blocks, controlled overbooking and visible confirmations.
bullets.three: Mobile access tuned for front-desk speed.

receipts.title: Cash and receipts with operational focus
receipts.subtitle: This module will complete the loop between service, checkout and daily close by branch.
receipts.description: The visual foundation is already ready for numbering, close-out and cashier states without feeling like a throwaway placeholder.

bullets.one: Branch numbering with a clear next-receipt view.
bullets.two: Daily summaries for reception and managers without duplicated navigation.
bullets.three: Empty and error states already match the rest of the product.

branches.title: Branches and multi-location operations
branches.subtitle: Branch administration will become the bridge between configuration, permissions and local execution.
branches.description: This placeholder already points toward the product direction: less cold backoffice, more actionable multi-branch context.

bullets.one: Policies, schedules and operational settings per branch.
bullets.two: Clear visibility into active, inactive and timezone-aware branches.
bullets.three: Direct integration with the top selector and user access model.

reports.title: Executive reporting with instant read
reports.subtitle: Built for managers who need business clarity without exporting spreadsheets.
reports.description: This module will consolidate KPIs, trends and branch comparisons in a surface that feels like SaaS product, not inherited backoffice.

bullets.one: Branch indicators, catalog performance and weekly trend reading.
bullets.two: Views ready for stakeholders without rebuilding filters each time.
bullets.three: Observability and business metrics connected in the same product layer.

notifications.title: Notifications and reminders hub
notifications.subtitle: The future experience will combine useful signals without turning into noisy inbox theater.
notifications.description: Appointment reminders, checkout alerts and internal nudges will live here so the team can operate with less friction.

bullets.one: Actionable reminders instead of a noisy notification tray.
bullets.two: Clear signals for schedule changes, checkout events and daily follow-up.
bullets.three: Designed to work well on mobile and front-desk flows.

settings.title: Settings with product intent
settings.subtitle: When this module lands, it should feel like a clear tenant control panel, not a junk drawer.
settings.description: The visual base already leaves room for tenant preferences, branding and operational settings that actually matter.

bullets.one: Branding, global schedules and tenant-level preferences.
bullets.two: Experience settings like theme, locale and operational behavior.
bullets.three: A space ready to grow without reworking the shell architecture.

meta.defaultTitle: BarberSuite | SaaS to run multi-branch barbershops
meta.description: Appointments, cash, reports, staff and notifications in one platform for barbershops that need operational clarity.

hero.title: BarberSuite runs the full barbershop without switching tools.
hero.subtitle: One system for appointments, cash, staff, reporting and notifications. Less improvised backoffice, more visible operations per branch.

preview.title: A shell designed for reception and managers
preview.calendarSubtitle: Backend-calculated slots and per-branch time zone.
preview.cashSubtitle: Charge, void and inspect detail without leaving the flow.
preview.reportSubtitle: Sales, top services and barber performance.

home.audienceTitle: Built for teams that need to operate, not improvise.
home.audienceSubtitle: BarberSuite is designed for owners, managers and front-desk teams that need branch-scoped control with less friction.

owners.title: Multi-branch owners
owners.description: Get visibility on cash, appointments and performance without relying on parallel spreadsheets or manual closeouts.

managers.title: Operating managers
managers.description: Resolve availability, no-shows, staff shifts and daily metrics from a single product surface.

teams.title: Reception and front desk
teams.description: Schedule, charge and follow clients with fewer screen hops and less room for manual errors.

finalCta.title: Turn appointments, checkout and reporting into a clean day-one operation.
finalCta.subtitle: Start with simple onboarding and grow into multiple branches without rebuilding architecture or internal workflows.

sections.featuresSubtitle: What you see on the marketing site already exists in product: appointments, checkout, reports, outbox and branch control.

agenda.title: Scheduling with real availability
agenda.description: Barbers, service slots and backend availability validation prevent appointments outside working hours.

cash.title: Checkout ready to operate
cash.description: Internal receipts per branch, branch numbering and controlled voiding with traceability.

reports.title: Reports that are actually readable
reports.description: Sales, top services, utilization and barber performance with range filters and real time zone handling.

notifications.title: Outbox and email without magic
notifications.description: Transactional confirmations and reminders with outbox, SMTP worker and observability.

multibranch.title: Multi-tenant + multi-branch
multibranch.description: Every module respects tenant, branch and RBAC boundaries so you can scale without leaking data.

rbac.title: Serious permissions and context
rbac.description: ADMIN, MANAGER, RECEPTION and BARBER only see what they need, with no unsafe shortcuts.

one.question: Does it work for more than one branch?
one.answer: Yes. The model is multi-tenant and branch-scoped, with a branch selector, permissions and numbering per location.

two.question: Does the scheduler prevent overlaps and off-hours appointments?
two.answer: Yes. The backend validates both overlap and effective barber availability before confirming an appointment.

three.question: Are checkout and receipts already operational?
three.answer: Yes. You can issue internal sales, void receipts and inspect branch history from the same UI.

four.question: What is next on the roadmap?
four.answer: WhatsApp, advanced availability improvements and later SRI/commercial automation steps.

meta.title: Features
meta.description: Appointments, checkout, reporting, notifications and multi-branch architecture for barbershops that need operational order.

featuresPage.title: Everything you can already operate today.
featuresPage.subtitle: A coherent stack for the full barbershop: staff, clients, checkout, scheduling and branch visibility.
featuresPage.architectureSubtitle: The product already sits on clear boundaries for tenant, branch, web security, outbox and observability.

agenda.title: Scheduling with context
agenda.description: Day/week/list views with availability, drag and drop and real validations.

bullets.one: Slots per barber and service from backend.
bullets.two: Protection against overlap and off-hours booking.
bullets.three: Confirmation and reminder flows ready to grow.

cash.title: Checkout by branch
cash.description: Internal sales, custom items, payments and controlled voiding with branch numbering.

bullets.one: Exact calculation with BigDecimal and validated formulas.
bullets.two: Receipt detail, filters and issued/voided states.
bullets.three: History ready for exports and reporting.

reports.title: Operational reporting
reports.description: Sales summary, top services, appointments and barber performance in one executive view.

bullets.one: Date ranges interpreted in the branch real time zone.
bullets.three: KPIs that help the team make same-day decisions.

notifications.title: Notifications with outbox
notifications.description: Reliable email pipeline with queues, retries and attempt tracing.

bullets.one: Admin API for send tests and auditability.
bullets.two: SMTP worker separated from the HTTP request path.
bullets.three: MailHog in demo and GreenMail in tests.

multibranch.title: Multi-branch operations
multibranch.description: Branch selector, branch-scoped modules and tenant-scoped administration.

bullets.one: Clients, appointments, checkout and reports live per branch.
bullets.two: Staff and services are managed at tenant level.
bullets.three: Same visual system ready for multiple locations.

security.title: Modern web security
security.description: BFF with httpOnly cookies, double-submit CSRF and origin validation.

bullets.two: Request IDs, JSON logs and business metrics included.
bullets.three: Consistent RBAC across backend and frontend.

tenancy.title: Real multi-tenancy
tenancy.description: Every query and constraint filters by tenant to prevent cross-business leaks.

branchScope.title: Branch scope where it matters
branchScope.description: Appointments, clients, checkout and reports require branch context and are enforced in backend.

outbox.title: Transactional outbox
outbox.description: Confirmations, reminders and send tests are persisted first and sent later by a worker.

observability.title: Integrated observability
observability.description: Actuator, Prometheus, Grafana, request IDs and business metrics from the start.

meta.title: Pricing
meta.description: Per-branch plans for appointments, checkout, reporting and staff, with a clear roadmap for advanced functions.

pricingPage.title: Clear per-branch pricing, no hidden complexity.
pricingPage.subtitle: Start with a solid operation and scale into multiple branches without rebuilding internal workflows.
pricingPage.roadmapSubtitle: What is already ready and what comes next in upcoming iterations.

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: For a barbershop that wants to leave operational chaos quickly.

pro.name: Pro
pro.description: For teams that already need multi-branch control and day-to-day visibility.

bullets.three: Staff admin, availability and full scheduling.
bullets.four: Multi-branch operations with one control plane.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For chains that need rollout, support and coordinated roadmap work.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp v1.1
whatsapp.description: Reminders and confirmations over conversational channels to reduce no-shows.

sri.title: SRI v1.1 / v2
sri.description: A later step from internal receipt to formal invoicing when the business needs it.

enterprise.title: Commercial automation
enterprise.description: More signals, segmentation and flows for retention, reactivation and client follow-up.

footer.rights: © BarberSuite. Serious operations for barbershops that grow.
